name: askii
description: ASKII is an AI code assistant with style ( •_•)>⌐■-■ (⌐■_■)

askii.llmPlatform.description: LLM platform to use (ollama, lmstudio, openai, anthropic, opencodego, or askiicloud)

askii.ollamaUrl.description: URL of the Ollama API server

askii.lmStudioUrl.description: URL of the LM Studio API server

askii.ollamaModel.description: Ollama model to use for explanations

askii.lmStudioModel.description: LM Studio model to use for explanations

askii.openaiApiKey.description: OpenAI API key (used when llmPlatform is openai)

askii.openaiModel.description: OpenAI model to use (e.g. gpt-5-mini, gpt-4o, gpt-4-turbo)

askii.openaiUrl.description: OpenAI-compatible base URL (leave empty for api.openai.com). Use this for Azure OpenAI or other compatible APIs.

askii.anthropicApiKey.description: Anthropic API key (used when llmPlatform is anthropic)

askii.anthropicModel.description: Anthropic model to use (e.g. claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5)

askii.opencodegoApiKey.description: opencode Go API key (used when llmPlatform is opencodego). Get it from https://opencode.ai/go

askii.opencodegoModel.description: opencode Go model id (e.g. glm-5.2, kimi-k2.7-code, deepseek-v4-pro, qwen3.7-max, minimax-m3). See https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/models

askii.opencodegoUrl.description: opencode Go base URL (OpenAI-compatible). Override only if needed.

askii.askiicloudApiKey.description: ASKII Cloud API key (used when llmPlatform is askiicloud).

askii.askiicloudModel.description: ASKII Cloud model id to use (when llmPlatform is askiicloud).

askii.askiicloudUrl.description: ASKII Cloud base URL (OpenAI-compatible). Override only if needed.

enumDescriptions: Show relevant wiki documentation snippets (requires askii.wikiPath to be indexed)
askii.inlineHelperMode.description: Inline helper mode for code decorations

askii.doMaxRounds.description: Maximum number of interaction rounds for ASKII Do command

askii.doAutoConfirm.description: Automatically confirm create/modify/delete actions in ASKII Do without prompting

askii.formatAfterEdit.description: Automatically format files after ASKII Edit or ASKII Do creates/modifies them

askii.browserHeadless.description: Launch the Puppeteer browser in headless mode for ASKII Browse (set to false to show the browser window)

askii.chromePath.description: Path to the Chrome/Chromium executable for ASKII Browse (e.g. C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe). Leave empty to use the system default.

askii.wikiEnabled.description: Enable wiki RAG context for Ask / Edit / Do commands. Requires askii.wikiPath to be set and the wiki to be indexed via ASKII: Reload Wiki.

askii.wikiPath.description: Path to folder containing .md documentation files used for wiki RAG context. Run 'ASKII: Reload Wiki' after changing this or updating the docs.

askii.wikiAutoReload.description: Automatically rebuild and reload the wiki index when the extension starts. Requires askii.wikiEnabled and askii.wikiPath to be configured.

askii.inlineCompletionEnabled.description: Enable ASKII inline code completion (Copilot-style ghost text in the editor; Tab to accept, Esc to dismiss).

enumDescriptions: Use the platform configured in askii.llmPlatform
askii.inlinePlatform.description: LLM platform to use for inline auto-complete and inline helper mode decorations. Set to "default" to follow askii.llmPlatform.

askii.inlineModel.description: Model id to use for inline auto-complete and helper mode. Set to "default" to use the default model of the selected platform (askii.ollamaModel, askii.openaiModel, etc.).

enumDescriptions: Trigger after 1200ms — fewer, more deliberate suggestions
askii.inlineCompletionEagerness.description: How eagerly ASKII requests inline completions. Controls the debounce delay and context window size.

askii.commitMessageInstructions.description: Path to a .md file with custom instructions for the commit message generator (injected as the system prompt, appended to the built-in rules). May be absolute or relative to the workspace root. Leave empty to use the built-in prompt.

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
